MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-12-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Billy Jones.
Gardiner McKleroy of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.

DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted:

Defendant Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing the Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to ask follow up questions about Bob Johnson, who Plaintiff testified made purchases with him at the Pacific Coast Building Supply Store. (Triplett Declaration, Ex. B at 667; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64. 81 [the court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered].) Defendant also did not submit the responses to its special interrogatories or request of production of documents with its moving papers, but attached the responses to Defendant Close Building Materials, Inc. (Triplett Declaration, Exs. I-L.) The Court did not consider the responses contained in Exhibits I-L of the Supplemental Amended Declaration of Gardiner McKleroy as they do not comply with the notice requirement under Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(a)(2). The declaration of Richard Bergeron is further insufficient to shift the burden as it does not address whether there was a Pacific Supply store operating in the Bay Area between 1960 and 1962.

Order signed in open court.
